codeReview.summary: LLM-based documentation generation implemented correctly in DocWriterAgent.execute(). The with_llm() constructor, load_system_prompt() method, and LLM call path are all implemented properly. Minor non-blocking issues noted.

issues.description: Variable 'prompt' is built from system_prompt_builder.build() but assigned to _prompt and never used when LLM path is taken. This appears to be dead code in the LLM execution path.

When changes_json.as_array() returns None or filtering produces no valid changes, an empty Vec<DocChange> is returned with success:true. This silently ignores parsing failures that might indicate LLM output issues.
Test test_doc_writer_agent uses DocWriterAgent::new() without LLM, so it continues to test stub mode only. The new LLM-based implementation path is not covered by tests.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker skill 'rust-worker' was referenced but does not exist in .factory/skills/. Worker proceeded without skill guidance.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows 'Skill "rust-worker" not found' error, then worker proceeded with implementation directly.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The project appears to have no convention for how agents should be wired with their LLM backends in production code. PlannerAgent/GeneratorAgent use with_llm() in tests with MockLlm, but there's no documented pattern for production agent instantiation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: DocWriterAgent::with_llm() exists but is never called in any production code path - only in this feature's implementation. Other agents like PlannerAgent similarly have with_llm() constructors not wired to production.

summary: Implementation passes review. DocWriterAgent.execute() now correctly implements LLM-based documentation generation by calling llm.chat() with messages built from the system prompt (loaded from .swell/prompts/doc_writer.md with fallback) and task context. The with_llm() constructor and load_system_prompt() method are properly implemented. Build and tests pass. Non-blocking issues: unused prompt variable in LLM path, silent empty response handling, and lack of test coverage for the LLM execution path.
